content:
Overview

Legacy 'Castle-and-Moat' VPNs grant excessive lateral trust once a perimeter is breached. This project deploys a Zero-Trust Network Access (ZTNA) solution that enforces 'Verify Explicitly' and 'Least Privilege' for administrative access to sensitive cloud infrastructure.

Technical Architecture

Protocol Optimization

Configured WireGuard (UDP) with a design target of ~80% handshake latency reduction vs. OpenVPN (unbenchmarked; target based on protocol characteristics, not measured on this deployment). Optimized MTU path discovery and deployed MSS clamping to prevent packet fragmentation across VPC peering links.

Identity Control Plane

Configured WireGuard peer identities with Active Directory (LDAP). Implemented instant access revocation across the entire fabric when an AD account is disabled.

Dark Infrastructure

Utilized AWS Security Groups and Network ACLs so the management subnet exposes zero public-facing listeners. Initiated connections via authenticated UDP hole-punching with persistent keep-alives.

Challenges

MTU & Fragmentation

Administrative tunnels often fail when crossing cloud provider boundaries due to 1500-byte packet limits.

Mitigated the failure mode by configuring PostUp iptables rules to clamp the Maximum Segment Size (MSS) to 1280 bytes, with the design intent of eliminating MTU-induced fragmentation on high-bandwidth telemetry paths.

Security Governance

NIST 800-207 Micro-segmentation

Built to satisfy NIST 800-207 standards. Implemented micro-segmentation where the Admin Peer can reach the DB-Proxy but never the DB-Master directly.

Outcomes

1 → 0Public administrative entry points by design (Bastion -> Dark Node)
<15msAdministrative session overhead (design target, unbenchmarked)
100%Compliance audit trail by design (every peer-to-peer transfer routed through centralized logging)

Before / After

BeforeInsecure Bastion Hosts with broad lateral trust once the perimeter is breached.
AfterIdentity-Based Tunnels enforcing Verify Explicitly and Least Privilege for every administrative connection.
